rationale: The citing paper is a meta-analysis of brand/SKU price elasticities from 81 studies using actual sales/scanner data, and the cited paper is an econometric store-level scanner study of own-brand price promotions with dynamic effects—an obvious topical/methodological overlap. Given the citing paper explicitly analyzes how modeling choices (e.g., promotion/price specification) affect elasticity estimates, this citation could be a substantive input or method source rather than a generic mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no contrary context, it is not safe to drop as incidental.
